mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy that lowers discount rates for equity valuation, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 230) signals central bank intervention risks, causing equity market volatility and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust supply-side expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant correlation with combined INDPRO/HOUST trends (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 7%) generates wage pressures that reduce corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 7%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer demand (RSAFS growth) and housing activity (HOUST) signal sustained economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no significant correlation with combined RSAFS/HOUST trends (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) indicates labor market strength that supports consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP (GDPC1 decline) during high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 230) signals stagflation risks, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no significant negative correlation with GDPC1/CPIAUCSL co-movements (Rank IC > -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Weak industrial production (INDPRO < 95) during a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) indicates slowing supply-side activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS/INDPRO co-movements (Rank IC > -0.05).
